title: Salesforce Flow: Maintain DTP User-Contact Relationship on Lead Conversion
description:
## Context

When a Salesforce lead is converted to a contact, any DataTools Pro user records associated with that lead must maintain their relationship with the newly created or existing contact. Without automation, DTP User records would only reference the lead and lose the connection to the contact.

## Behavior

Build a Salesforce record-triggered flow on the Lead object that fires on update when `IsConverted` becomes true. The flow:

1. Captures the `ConvertedContactId` from the lead record
2. Finds all DataTools Pro User records related to the lead
3. Updates each DTP User record's `Contact__c` lookup with the converted contact ID

This ensures DTP Users are related to both the lead and the contact after conversion.

## Test Cases

- **Test 1**: Lead with 1 DTP User converts to new contact — DTP User gets stamped with contact
- **Test 2**: Lead with 2 DTP Users converts — both DTP Users get stamped
- **Test 3**: Lead converts and merges into existing contact — DTP Users get stamped with the merge target contact

## Deployment

Implemented in sandbox first, then deployed to production via change set. This established the pattern for sandbox-to-production deployments via change sets.
acceptance_criteria: Flow triggers on lead conversion (IsConverted = true)
All DataTools Pro User records related to the converted lead are updated with the Contact__c lookup
Works correctly when lead has a single DTP User
Works correctly when lead has multiple DTP Users
Works correctly when lead is merged into an existing contact
DTP User retains both Lead__c and Contact__c relationships after conversion
Flow is deployed via change set from sandbox to production
middleware_changes: Salesforce record-triggered flow on the Lead object (fires on update when IsConverted = true). Uses an assignment element to capture ConvertedContactId, then updates all related DataTools Pro User records' Contact__c field. Flow was named 'Update datatools user contact on Lead conversion'.
implementation_notes: Salesforce deprecated Process Builder in favor of Flows, so this was built as a record-triggered flow. Encountered a permissions issue on the sandbox where the flow could not update the DTP User object — resolved by adjusting permissions. The flow was deployed from sandbox to production via change set, establishing the deployment pattern for future PBO work.
comment_summary: Developer built the flow on a Trailhead org first, then moved to the sandbox. Hit a permissions issue preventing DTP User updates during lead conversion, which was resolved. The flow was deployed to production via change set after sandbox validation.
